name: Lid-Driven Cavity
description: Classic benchmark: flow in a square cavity driven by a moving lid. The simplest CFD case — perfect for learning mesh generation, boundary conditions, and running a solver.
learning_objectives: Set velocity and pressure boundary conditions

mesh.title: Generate Mesh
mesh.description: Create a 2D square mesh using blockMesh. The cavity is a unit square (1m x 1m) divided into a uniform grid.
mesh.tip: Try changing the block resolution from (20 20 1) to (40 40 1) to see how mesh refinement affects results.

boundaries.title: Set Boundary Conditions
boundaries.description: Configure the velocity (U) and pressure (p) at each wall. The top wall moves at 1 m/s — this drives the flow.
boundaries.tip: The movingWall has a fixedValue velocity of (1 0 0). The other walls use noSlip (zero velocity).

solver.title: Configure Solver
solver.description: Set the time stepping, output frequency, and numerical schemes. icoFoam solves incompressible laminar Navier-Stokes equations.
solver.tip: deltaT controls the time step size. Smaller values are more stable but take longer.

run.title: Run Simulation
run.description: Execute icoFoam to solve the flow field. Watch the residuals — they should decrease over time, indicating convergence.
run.tip: If residuals plateau or increase, the simulation may be diverging. Try reducing deltaT.

postprocess.title: View Results
postprocess.description: The simulation is complete. Open in ParaView to visualize the velocity field and pressure distribution.
postprocess.tip: In ParaView, apply a 'Glyph' filter to U to see velocity vectors, or use 'Contour' on p for pressure.
